analysis.background: Delayed recognition of aerosol transmission as the primary mode of SARS‑CoV‑2 spread highlighted the need to understand aerosol mechanisms for other pathogens, motivating investigation of Andes virus on a cruise ship.
analysis.methods: The authors highlight epidemiological details of Andes virus transmission on the MV Hondius cruise ship, but specific study design, sample size, population, and laboratory methods are not reported in the abstract.
analysis.results: Not reported in the supplied evidence.
analysis.contribution: The paper proposes that aerosol spread may be a key mechanism for Andes virus transmission in permissive environments, suggesting implications for surveillance and infection‑control strategies on ships.
analysis.limitations: Only the abstract is available; detailed methods, quantitative results, and formal limitations are not reported.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Delayed recognition of aerosol transmission of SARS‑CoV‑2 underscored the importance of aerosol spread for emerging viruses. Methods: The abstract highlights epidemiological details of Andes virus on a cruise ship, but specific design and data are not reported. Results: Not reported. Contribution: Authors suggest aerosol spread may be a key mechanism for Andes virus, informing surveillance and control on ships. Limitations: Only the abstract is available; detailed methods, results, and limitations are absent.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 在邮轮上:COVID-19 的教训。

analysis_zh.background: 对气溶胶传播作为SARS‐CoV‐2主要传播方式的迟迟认识,凸显了了解其他病原体气溶胶机制的必要性,促使对航船上安第斯病毒的研究。
analysis_zh.methods: 作者强调了在MV Hondius邮轮上安第斯病毒传播的流行病学细节,但摘要未报告具体的研究设计、样本量、研究人群和实验室方法。
analysis_zh.results: 所提供的证据中未报告。
analysis_zh.contribution: 本文提出气溶胶传播可能是安第斯病毒在适宜环境中传播的关键机制,暗示对船舶监测和感染控制策略的意义。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要;未报告详细方法、定量结果和正式的局限性。

papers.title: IMMUNOINFORMATICS-GUIDED MULTI-EPITOPE VACCINE CANDIDATE DESIGN AND MHC DOCKING VALIDATION TARGETING THE GLYCOPROTEIN PRECURSOR OF THE NEWLY REPORTED ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 ORTHOHANTAVIRUS
papers.abstract: Abstract The rapid release of viral genomic sequences allows early immunoinformatics screening before experimentally curated antigen information becomes available. In this study, the glycoprotein precursor (GP) of the newly reported ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 orthohantavirus was evaluated as a vaccine-design target using a stepwise workflow that integrated antigenicity prediction, conserved-region screening, transmembrane topology, MHC-I and MHC-II epitope prediction, peptide-construct design, and molecular docking against representative MHC receptors. The full GP sequence was predicted as a probable antigen by VaxiJen with a score of 0.5194. Conserved regions 1-50 and 81-240 were retained as antigenic regions and were located outside the predicted membrane-spanning helices. After filtering by MHC binding score and independent VaxiJen antigenicity, two CTL epitopes (VIYEKTHCV and KVKKTVLCY) and six HTL epitopes were selected. Four multi-epitope constructs (F1-F4) were generated using AAY and GPGPG linkers. Docking showed that F4 had the strongest MHC-I docking score (-226.92), whereas F2 had the strongest MHC-II docking score (-260.14). Residue contact-map analysis further indicated that the strongest complexes were supported by hydrogen bonds, salt bridges, alkyl/aliphatic contacts, and van der Waals interactions. These findings prioritize F2 and F4 as leading candidates for further three-dimensional refinement, molecular dynamics, allergenicity/toxicity filtering, population coverage, and experimental validation.
papers.journal: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

analysis.background: The study addresses the need for rapid vaccine candidate identification for the newly reported ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 orthohantavirus, leveraging the quick availability of viral genome sequences.
analysis.methods: The authors applied an immunoinformatics workflow that included antigenicity prediction (VaxiJen), conserved‑region screening, transmembrane topology analysis, MHC‑I and MHC‑II epitope prediction, peptide‑construct design with AAY/GPGPG linkers, and molecular docking against representative MHC receptors.
analysis.results: The GP protein scored 0.5194 in VaxiJen, with antigenic conserved regions 1‑50 and 81‑240 outside membrane helices; two CTL and six HTL epitopes were selected, yielding four constructs (F1‑F4); docking identified F4 as best for MHC‑I (‑226.92) and F2 as best for MHC‑II (‑260.14), with interaction analyses showing hydrogen bonds, salt bridges, alkyl/aliphatic contacts, and van der Waals forces.
analysis.contribution: The work provides a prioritized set of multi‑epitope vaccine candidates (F2, F4) and demonstrates a rapid in‑silico pipeline for orthohantavirus vaccine design, guiding subsequent refinement and experimental testing.
analysis.limitations: Only abstract data are provided; no experimental validation, population‑coverage analysis, or allergenicity/toxicity assessment is reported.

analysis.summary_en: Background: The study targets rapid vaccine design for the newly reported ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 orthohantavirus using early genomic data. Methods: An immunoinformatics pipeline integrated VaxiJen antigenicity, conserved‑region screening, transmembrane topology, MHC‑I/II epitope prediction, construct design with linkers, and MHC docking. Results: GP scored 0.5194; antigenic regions 1‑50 and 81‑240 were selected, yielding two CTL and six HTL epitopes assembled into four constructs (F1‑F4). Docking ranked F4 highest for MHC‑I (‑226.92) and F2 for MHC‑II (‑260.14), with supportive hydrogen bonds, salt bridges, and van der Waals contacts. Contribution: Prioritizes F2 and F4 as vaccine candidates and showcases a fast in‑silico design workflow. Limitations: Findings are limited to abstract‑only information; no experimental or broader immunological validation is presented.

papers.title_zh: 免疫信息学指导的多表位疫苗候选设计及MHC对接验证,针对新报告的安第斯病毒/瑞士/Hu-3337/2026 糖蛋白前体 正汉坦病毒

analysis_zh.background: 本研究针对新报告的安第斯病毒/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 正汉坦病毒,满足快速筛选疫苗候选的需求,利用病毒基因组序列的快速获取。
analysis_zh.methods: 作者采用免疫信息学工作流,包括抗原性预测(VaxiJen)、保守区筛选、跨膜拓扑分析、MHC‐I 与 MHC‐II 表位预测、使用 AAY/GPGPG 连接肽的构建设计,以及对代表性 MHC 受体的分子对接。
analysis_zh.results: GP 蛋白在 VaxiJen 中得分 0.5194,抗原性保守区为 1‐50 和 81‐240,位于膜螺旋之外;筛选出两段 CTL 表位和六段 HTL 表位,构建出四个构体(F1‐F4);对接结果显示 F4 在 MHC‐I 上得分最佳(‐226.92),F2 在 MHC‐II 上得分最佳(‐260.14),相互作用分析揭示氢键、盐桥、烷基/脂肪族接触以及范德华力。
analysis_zh.contribution: 该工作提供了优先的多表位疫苗候选(F2、F4),并展示了用于 正汉坦病毒 疫苗设计的快速体外计算流程,为后续的优化和实验验证提供指导。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要数据;未报告实验验证、人口覆盖率分析或致敏性/毒性评估。

papers.summary_zh: 背景:本研究针对新报告的安第斯病毒/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 正汉坦病毒,满... 方法:作者采用免疫信息学工作流,包括抗原性预测(VaxiJen)、保守区筛选、跨膜拓扑分析、MHC‐... 结果:GP 蛋白在 VaxiJen 中得分 0.5194,抗原性保守区为 1‐50 和 81‐240... 贡献:该工作提供了优先的多表位疫苗候选(F2、F4),并展示了用于 正汉坦病毒 疫苗设计的快速体外计... 局限:仅提供摘要数据;未报告实验验证、人口覆盖率分析或致敏性/毒性评估。

papers.title: Hantavirus infections in a changing world: epidemiology, pathogenesis, and one health challenges.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics. Transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, hantaviruses cause two major clinical syndromes: haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS). Despite being historically confined to specific geographic regions, recent environmental changes, increased human animal interaction, and global mobility have contributed to evolving epidemiological patterns. This review summarises current knowledge on hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiological mechanisms, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches. Particular attention is given to the emerging global epidemiology of Seoul virus (SEOV), an urban-associated hantavirus linked to brown rats and pet rodents. In addition, it explores emerging challenges related to vaccine development, surveillance, and the integration of One Health strategies in the prevention and control of hantavirus infections. Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection remain critical to mitigating the global impact of these infections.

analysis.background: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics.
analysis.main_directions: They are transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, causing haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS), with emerging urban-associated Seoul virus (SEOV) linked to brown rats and pet rodents.
analysis.current_state: Recent environmental changes, increased human–animal interaction, and global mobility have shifted epidemiological patterns, and current knowledge covers transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiology, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches.
analysis.gaps: Challenges remain in vaccine development, surveillance, and integrating One Health strategies for prevention and control.
analysis.future_research: Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection are critical to mitigating the global impact of hantavirus infections.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics. Main directions: They are transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, causing haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS), with emerging urban-associated Seoul virus (SEOV) linked to brown rats and pet rodents. Current state: Recent environmental changes, increased human–animal interaction, and global mobility have shifted epidemiological patterns, and current knowledge covers transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiology, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches. Gaps: Challenges remain in vaccine development, surveillance, and integrating One Health strategies for prevention and control. Future research: Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection are critical to mitigating the global impact of hantavirus infections.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 变化中的世界中的感染:流行病学、发病机制与一体化健康挑战。

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒感染是具有全球相关性的动物源性疾病,特征为显著的发病率和死亡率以及复杂的生态动态。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 它们主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播,引起肾综合征出血热 (HFRS) 和 汉坦病毒心肺综合征 (HCPS),并出现与城市环境相关的 首尔病毒 (SEOV),该病毒与褐鼠和宠物啮齿动物有关。
analysis_zh.current_state: 近期的环境变化、人畜互动增加以及全球流动性已改变流行病学模式,当前的认识包括传播途径、全球流行病学、发病机制、临床表现和治疗方法。
analysis_zh.gaps: 在疫苗研发、监测以及将One Health策略整合用于预防和控制方面仍面临挑战。
analysis_zh.future_research: 加强多学科方法并提升早期检测对于减轻 汉坦病毒感染的全球影响至关重要。

papers.title: Hantavirus evidence in West and Central Asia: a scoping review protocol with implications for surveillance
papers.abstract: Hantaviruses are hosted mainly by rodents and other small mammals. Human infection may present as haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome, nephropathia epidemica, hantavirus pulmonary syndrome, or hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome, although mild and non-specific febrile illness also occurs. Patterns of disease and viral circulation depend on the virus and host species, local ecology, human exposure, diagnostic practice, and the organisation of surveillance. Evidence from West and Central Asia is scattered across clinical reports, serological surveys, rodent studies, molecular investigations, and surveillance programmes for undifferentiated febrile illness. The available literature suggests that hantaviruses circulate in parts of Turkey, Iran, Kazakhstan, and the South Caucasus, but the amount and type of evidence vary markedly between countries. In several settings, detection has relied on a single serological assay, while molecular confirmation and linked human-animal surveillance remain uncommon. Other countries in the region have little or no readily identifiable published evidence. A lack of publications, however, cannot be taken to mean that viral circulation is absent. A scoping review is suited to this heterogeneous literature. The purpose is to establish what has been studied, where evidence has been generated, how infection or circulation has been assessed, and which gaps are most relevant to surveillance.

analysis.background: Hantavirus evidence in West and Central Asia is fragmented across clinical, serological, rodent, molecular, and surveillance reports, prompting a review to inform surveillance.
analysis.main_directions: The review will map disease manifestations (HF with renal syndrome, HPS, etc.), ecological drivers (virus‑host species, local ecology, exposure, diagnostics, surveillance), and the suitability of a scoping approach for heterogeneous literature.
analysis.current_state: Published data indicate hantavirus circulation in Turkey, Iran, Kazakhstan, and the South Caucasus, though evidence quantity and type differ markedly among countries; many detections rely on single serological assays with limited molecular confirmation or linked human‑animal studies.
analysis.gaps: Molecular confirmation and integrated human‑animal surveillance are uncommon; several countries have scant or no published data; lack of publications does not prove absence of circulation.
analysis.future_research: The scoping review will delineate studied topics, geographic coverage, assessment methods, and prioritize surveillance gaps to guide targeted epidemiological and ecological investigations.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantavirus evidence in West and Central Asia is fragmented across clinical, serological, rodent, molecular, and surveillance reports, prompting a review to inform surveillance. Main directions: The review will map disease manifestations (HF with renal syndrome, HPS, etc.), ecological drivers (virus‑host species, local ecology, exposure, diagnostics, surveillance), and the suitability of a scoping approach for heterogeneous literature. Current state: Published data indicate hantavirus circulation in Turkey, Iran, Kazakhstan, and the South Caucasus, though evidence quantity and type differ markedly among countries; many detections rely on single serological assays with limited molecular confirmation or linked human‑animal studies. Gaps: Molecular confirmation and integrated human‑animal surveillance are uncommon; several countries have scant or no published data; lack of publications does not prove absence of circulation. Future research: The scoping review will delineate studied topics, geographic coverage, assessment methods, and prioritize surveillance gaps to guide targeted epidemiological and ecological investigations.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 在西部和中亚的证据:一项范围审查协议,涉及监测的意义

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒 在西亚和中亚的证据分散于临床、血清学、啮齿动物、分子和监测报告中,促使开展综述以为监测提供信息。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 本综述将绘制疾病表现(肾综合征出血热、汉坦病毒肺综合征等)、生态驱动因素(病毒‐宿主种类、当地生态、暴露、诊断、监测),并评估范围性方法在异质文献中的适用性。
analysis_zh.current_state: 已发表的数据表明 汉坦病毒 在土耳其、伊朗、哈萨克斯坦和南高加索存在循环,尽管各国的证据数量和类型差异显著;许多检测依赖单一血清学检测,缺乏充分的分子确认或人畜关联研究。
analysis_zh.gaps: 分子确认和人畜一体化监测较为罕见;部分国家的已发表数据稀少或缺失;出版物的缺乏并不证明不存在循环。
analysis_zh.future_research: 本范围性综述将阐明研究主题、地域覆盖、评估方法,并优先列出监测空白,以指导有针对性的流行病学和生态学调查。

papers.title: ANALYSIS OF GENOTYPE FREQUENCIES OF MONONUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS OF EARLY IMMUNE RESPONSE GENES IN HFRS PATIENTS
papers.abstract: Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) remains one of the leading natural focal infections in Russia, characterized by pronounced clinical variability. Clinical manifestations of HFRS, in particular, may be determined by single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in early immune response genes in patients. We determined the allele and genotype frequencies of SNPs in the CASP1 (rs580253) and IL‐6 (rs1800795) genes. CASP1 encodes caspase‐1, an enzyme that is the product of the activated inflammasome, while IL‐6 encodes interleukin‐6, a cytokine of the acute immune response. It was found that the genotype frequencies in the control group conformed to the Hardy–Weinberg law, whereas in HFRS patients a statistically significant deviation from the expected equilibrium was detected, which suggests the existence of correlations between the genotypes of the studied SNPs and the clinical manifestations of the disease.

analysis.background: Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) is a leading natural focal infection in Russia with pronounced clinical variability.
analysis.methods: The study determined allele and genotype frequencies of SNPs in the CASP1 (rs580253) and IL‑6 (rs1800795) genes, as clinical manifestations of HFRS may be influenced by such polymorphisms.
analysis.results: In HFRS patients, genotype frequencies deviated significantly from Hardy–Weinberg equilibrium, whereas the control group conformed to the expected equilibrium, indicating a potential association between these SNPs and disease manifestations.
analysis.contribution: The findings suggest that early immune response gene polymorphisms may contribute to the clinical variability observed in HFRS patients.
analysis.limitations: Not reported in the supplied evidence; the study is presented as a conference abstract with limited methodological detail.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) is a leading natural focal infection in Russia with pronounced clinical variability. Methods: The study determined allele and genotype frequencies of SNPs in the CASP1 (rs580253) and IL‑6 (rs1800795) genes, as clinical manifestations of HFRS may be influenced by such polymorphisms. Results: In HFRS patients, genotype frequencies deviated significantly from Hardy–Weinberg equilibrium, whereas the control group conformed to the expected equilibrium, indicating a potential association between these SNPs and disease manifestations. Contribution: The findings suggest that early immune response gene polymorphisms may contribute to the clinical variability observed in HFRS patients. Limitations: Not reported in the supplied evidence; the study is presented as a conference abstract with limited methodological detail.

papers.title_zh: HFRS患者早期免疫应答基因单核苷酸多态性基因型频率分析

analysis_zh.background: 肾综合征出血热(肾综合征出血热 (HFRS))是俄罗斯主要的自然灶感染,临床变异显著。
analysis_zh.methods: 本研究测定了CASP1 (rs580253) 和 IL-6 (rs1800795) 基因中SNP的等位基因和基因型频率,因为肾综合征出血热的临床表现可能受这些多态性的影响。
analysis_zh.results: 在肾综合征出血热患者中,基因型频率显著偏离哈迪-温伯格平衡,而对照组符合预期平衡,提示这些SNP可能与疾病表现相关。
analysis_zh.contribution: 研究结果提示,早期免疫应答基因多态性可能导致肾综合征出血热患者的临床变异。
analysis_zh.limitations: 在提供的证据中未报告;该研究以会议摘要形式呈现,方法细节有限。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：肾综合征出血热(肾综合征出血热 (HFRS))是俄罗斯主要的自然灶感染,临床变异显著。 方法：本研究测定了CASP1 (rs580253) 和 IL-6 (rs1800795) 基因中SNP的等位基因和基因型频率,因为肾综合征出血热的临… 结果：在肾综合征出血热患者中,基因型频率显著偏离哈迪-温伯格平衡,而对照组符合预期平衡,提示这些SNP可能与疾病表现相关。 贡献：研究结果提示,早期免疫应答基因多态性可能导致肾综合征出血热患者的临床变异。 局限：在提供的证据中未报告;该研究以会议摘要形式呈现,方法细节有限。

papers.title: A 5-Adic Ultrametric Framework for Alignment-Free Phylogenetic Analysis of Hantavirus RNA Sequences
papers.abstract: We develop a non-Archimedean framework for the representation and analysis of genomic sequences based on the arithmetic and geometric structure of the ring of 5-adic integers. The proposed approach associates RNA sequences with points in a compact ultrametric space through an injective symbolic-to-arithmetic embedding that transforms genomic information into a hierarchical geometric object. We prove that the embedding is a global isometry between a natural symbolic prefix metric and the induced 5-adic metric, and we show that its image forms a compact Cantor-type subset of Z5. Building upon this representation, we formulate a continuous-time evolutionary model governed by a Vladimirov pseudo-differential operator. The resulting non-Archimedean diffusion equation provides a mathematically rigorous mechanism for describing evolutionary transitions across hierarchical genomic scales and admits an explicit fundamental solution obtained through 5-adic Fourier analysis. We further introduce a finite-resolution projection onto quotient rings of Z5 and develop an alignment-free phylogenetic inference framework based directly on the 5-adic valuation. The induced distance function is ultrametric and naturally encodes hierarchical relationships through shared symbolic prefixes. The proposed construction establishes a bridge between p-adic analysis, ultrametric geometry, pseudo-differential operators, and computational phylogenetics. As an illustration, we discuss its application to Hantavirus genomic sequences, demonstrating how hierarchical evolutionary organization can be represented within a unified non-Archimedean mathematical framework.

analysis.background: The paper tackles the need for alignment‑free phylogenetic methods for viral RNA, exemplified by Hantavirus, by proposing a non‑Archimedean mathematical framework.
analysis.methods: RNA sequences are mapped to points in the 5‑adic integer ring via an injective symbolic‑to‑arithmetic embedding, with finite‑resolution projection onto quotient rings and a 5‑adic valuation‑based ultrametric distance used for phylogenetic inference.
analysis.results: The embedding is proved to be a global isometry whose image forms a compact Cantor‑type subset of Z5; a Vladimirov pseudo‑differential diffusion equation with an explicit fundamental solution is derived, and the induced ultrametric distance naturally encodes hierarchical relationships, illustrated on Hantavirus genomes.
analysis.contribution: Establishes a novel bridge between p‑adic analysis, ultrametric geometry, and computational phylogenetics, offering a mathematically rigorous alignment‑free framework for hierarchical evolutionary analysis.
analysis.limitations: Only an abstract is provided; no empirical validation, performance comparison, or detailed data are reported.

analysis.summary_en: Background: The study addresses the need for alignment‑free phylogenetic analysis of viral RNA, using Hantavirus as an example. Methods: RNA sequences are embedded into the 5‑adic integer ring via an injective symbolic‑to‑arithmetic map, with finite‑resolution projection and a 5‑adic valuation‑based ultrametric distance for inference. Results: The embedding is a global isometry forming a compact Cantor‑type subset; a Vladimirov pseudo‑differential diffusion equation with an explicit solution is derived, and the ultrametric distance encodes hierarchical relationships, demonstrated on Hantavirus genomes. Contribution: Provides a novel, mathematically rigorous bridge between p‑adic analysis and computational phylogenetics for alignment‑free evolutionary studies. Limitations: Only an abstract is available; no empirical validation or detailed data are reported.

papers.title_zh: 一种用于无序列比对的 汉坦病毒 RNA 序列系统发育分析的 5‐adic 超度量框架

analysis_zh.background: 本文针对病毒RNA的无比对系统发育方法需求(以 汉坦病毒 为例),提出了一种非阿基米德数学框架。
analysis_zh.methods: RNA序列通过注入式符号到算术的嵌入映射到5进整数环中的点,并在商环上进行有限分辨率投影,利用基于5进估值的超度量距离进行系统发育推断。
analysis_zh.results: 证明该嵌入为全局等距映射,其像构成Z5中的紧致Cantor型子集;推导出具有显式基本解的Vladimirov伪微分扩散方程;诱导的超度量距离自然编码层级关系,并在 汉坦病毒 基因组上进行示例说明。
analysis_zh.contribution: 在p进分析、超度量几何与计算系统发育之间建立新桥梁,提供了数学上严谨的无比对层级进化分析框架。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要;未报告实证验证、性能比较或详细数据。

papers.title: Watching the Ship, Missing the Forest: Why Ecological Spillover, Reservoir Ecology, and Environmental Change Remain the True Hantavirus Threats in North America.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus emergence in North America is driven by interactions among reservoir ecology, environmental variability, landscape conditions, and human exposure, with ongoing environmental change increasing spillover opportunities, altering the geography of risk, and challenging traditional public health surveillance systems.

papers.title: Serologic Evidence of Hantavirus Exposure in Humans, Italy, 2004-2018.
papers.abstract: Rodentborne hantaviruses and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV) circulate in northern Italy but are rarely diagnosed. We tested samples collected from 371 patients during 2004-2018 and detected LCMV IgG in 2.7% of samples and hantavirus IgG in 7.3%. Puumala virus-neutralizing antibodies were confirmed in 10 cases, indicating unrecognized local hantavirus circulation.

papers.full_text: Disclaimer: Early release articles are not considered as final versions. Any changes will be reflected in the online version in the month the article is officially released. Volume 32, Number 9—September 2026 Dispatch Serologic Evidence of Hantavirus Exposure in Humans, Italy, 2004–2018 Suggested citation for this article Abstract Rodentborne hantaviruses and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV) circulate in northern Italy but are rarely diagnosed. We tested samples collected from 371 patients during 2004–2018 and detected LCMV IgG in 2.7% of samples and hantavirus IgG in 7.3%. Puumala virus–neutralizing antibodies were confirmed in 10 cases, indicating unrecognized local hantavirus circulation. In Europe, endemic rodentborne viruses include Old World hantaviruses (Hantaviridae) and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV; Arenaviridae) (1). Puumala virus (PUUV), carried by the bank vole (Clethrionomys glareolus), is the most widespread hantavirus in Europe. In southeastern Europe and the Balkans, Dobrava-Belgrade virus (DOBV) in yellow-necked mice (Apodemus flavicollis), and Saaremaa virus in striped field mice (A. agrarius) are also present (1). Those mouse species and other potential reservoir species, such as the Mus musculus mouse and Rattus norvegicus rat, are common in northern Italy, including the regions discussed in this article (2). DOBV and PUUV cause hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS), and LCMV, transmitted by house mice (M. musculus), can cause febrile illness or aseptic meningitis (1). Unlike in many neighboring countries, rodentborne infections are not commonly diagnosed in Italy, although evidence of LCMV and DOBV circulation and human exposure exists (2–6). This study aimed to determine serologic evidence of hantaviruses and LCMV exposure in northwestern Italy, a region where these viruses have not been extensively studied. The study samples (n = 371, average age 59.9 years) were collected during 2004–2018 for other studies in the provinces of Novara, Biella, Vercelli, and Verbano Cusio-Ossola (Figure) and included patients with various chronic diseases, including nephrologic pathologies, alcohol abuse, hepatitis C virus infection, and liver diseases. All patients provided written informed consent, and the study was approved by the local ethics committee. In 2018, we screened patient plasma samples for PUUV, DOBV and LCMV IgG using immunofluorescence assay (IFA) as previously described (2). To further determine hantavirus antibody specificity, we tested neutralizing antibodies in 2024 and 2025 against PUUV and DOBV for samples with IgG titers >1:40 in DOBV or PUUV IgG IFA. We performed focus reduction neutralization tests (FRNTs) in Vero E6 cells according to previously published protocols (7,8). In brief, we incubated Vero E6 cells with serially diluted plasma samples and hantavirus (50 focus-forming units/well) in 24-well plates. After adsorption, we overlaid cells with MEM-agarose and incubated for 8–10 days, depending on the virus. We detected foci by immunostaining with a hantavirus-specific monoclonal antibody and horseradish peroxidase–conjugated secondary antibody, visualized with AEC (3-amino-9-ethylcarbazole) substrate. We determined neutralizing titers on the basis of the reduction in foci relative to virus-only controls; we used an 80% reduction relative to the negative control as the cutoff for enhanced specificity. Of 371 patients, 27 (7.3% [95% Wilson CI 5.0%–10.4%]) were positive for hantavirus IgG by IFA (titers 1:20–1:640) (Table 1). We further tested 11 with titers >1:40 by FRNT and detected PUUV-neutralizing activity in 10/11 of the tested samples (titers 1:80 to >1:320). Neutralization of DOBV was generally lower (titers <1:40 to 1:80). Several samples exhibited PUUV-specific neutralization with minimal or absent DOBV-neutralizing activity (Table 2). For LCMV, 10 of 371 patients (2.7% [95% Wilson CI 1.5%–4.9%]) were IgG positive (titers 1:20 to 1:40) (Table 1). Seropositive cases were geographically dispersed (Figure), and antibodies to both viruses were detected only in the region of Novara. This study detected antibodies against hantaviruses and arenaviruses in patients from a previously unstudied region of northwestern Italy. Hantavirus infections are routinely reported in neighboring countries but are rarely diagnosed in Italy; yearly cases have been reported in France, Austria, and Slovenia (9). LCMV seroprevalence (2.7%) matched previous reports from the Trentino area (2). Our hantavirus seropositivity (7.3%) was higher than the 0.2% DOBV IgG reported for forestry workers in Trentino in 2002 (2) and above the estimated 1.7% average in the general population in Italy (10). Higher seroprevalence (up to 8.8%) has been reported in occupationally rodent-exposed risk groups (5). IFA results demonstrated that plasma samples were positive for PUUV or for DOBV IgG; however, FRNT demonstrated predominantly PUUV-specific neutralizing activity, suggesting PUUV as the likely infecting virus. Neutralization against DOBV was absent or detected at low titers, consistent with serologic cross-reactivity rather than an actual DOBV infection. Only 1 sample (S52) showed reactivity to both PUUV and DOBV antigens by IFA and had a mixed neutralization profile. That finding could indicate exposure to an antigenically related hantavirus or the presence of cross-reactive antibodies. Cross-neutralizing antibody responses among hantaviruses have been documented in both acute and late-convalescent patient samples, potentially complicating serologic interpretation. Although dual exposure to both PUUV and DOBV cannot be excluded, the serologic findings do not enable a definite distinction between these possibilities (7,11) The first limitation of our study is that single-time-point sampling precluded infection timing and symptom linkage to seropositivity. Because samples were collected from chronically ill persons, the findings might not fully represent the general population, and data on risk factors, residence, and travel history were unavailable. Residence in a large metropolitan area such as Milan could argue against local transmission; however, patients’ chronic conditions and regular follow-up at the regional university hospital support predominantly local or regional exposure. Furthermore, geographic representation was uneven; most patients resided in the Novara area, potentially biasing the spatial distribution of seropositive cases. Overall, we found no clear association between serologic findings and patient group, location, or sampling timing. Most hantavirus-seropositive patients were men 36–88 years of age, consistent with reports of higher incidence of hantavirus infection in men and a predominance in the 45–64–year age group (9). The neutralization profile strongly suggested exposure to PUUV or a closely related hantavirus rather than DOBV, previously reported in Italy (2,4–6). The symptoms of PUUV and DOBV infections can vary from asymptomatic to severe and often include fever, headache, abdominal pain, backache, and nausea, occurring in the absence of respiratory symptoms (1). Although both viruses can cause HFRS, disease associated with DOBV is more severe (5%–10% fatality rate), whereas PUUV infection is typically milder (<1% fatality rate) (1), which might hinder clinical recognition. PUUV infections have been documented in neighboring countries in the Alpine region and central Europe (12–14) and, given the continuity of forest ecosystems and the distribution of the bank vole, its presence in northern Italy is plausible. Although the bank vole, the natural host of PUUV, typically inhabits forests, our study area was largely semiurban and agricultural with fragmented woodland. How human exposure occurs in such environments warrants further investigation. Of note, a recent PUUV outbreak in coastal Croatia (15) highlights the virus’s ability to emerge beyond typical bank vole habitats. Our serologic findings in a cohort of chronically ill patients in northern Italy demonstrated exposure to hantaviruses and LCMV. The rate of hantavirus antibody detection was notably high, suggesting local transmission. Together with previous reports from Italy, our findings highlight that rodentborne viruses, particularly hantaviruses, are likely endemic beyond the currently recognized regions. Because of limited data on human infections, hantaviruses remain largely underdiagnosed and neglected in Italy. Although the most recent samples in this study date from 2018, the detection of antibodies across multiple sampling years suggests sustained exposure in this region. Further studies in both rodents and humans are needed to identify endemic hantavirus species and better estimate associated risks and disease burden. Raising clinician awareness of mild forms of HFRS, such as nephropathia epidemica, and ensuring access to appropriate laboratory diagnostics are crucial for timely detection and confirmation of infections. Public health messaging would be essential to minimize exposure to rodents and their excreta, helping to prevent rodentborne diseases across Italy. Dr. Faolotto is a physician affiliated with the University Hospital in Novara, Italy, where she combines clinical practice with research. Her work has included contributions to research in infectious diseases and epidemiology, with a focus on advancing clinical knowledge and patient care. Acknowledgment This study was supported by grant from FreeMover of the Università del Piemonte Orientale, Sigrid Jusélius Foundation, Jane and Aatos Erkko Foundation, and the Research Council of Finland. full_text_sections.full_text: Disclaimer: Early release articles are not considered as final versions. Any changes will be reflected in the online version in the month the article is officially released. Volume 32, Number 9—September 2026 Dispatch Serologic Evidence of Hantavirus Exposure in Humans, Italy, 2004–2018 Suggested citation for this article Abstract Rodentborne hantaviruses and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV) circulate in northern Italy but are rarely diagnosed. We tested samples collected from 371 patients during 2004–2018 and detected LCMV IgG in 2.7% of samples and hantavirus IgG in 7.3%. Puumala virus–neutralizing antibodies were confirmed in 10 cases, indicating unrecognized local hantavirus circulation. In Europe, endemic rodentborne viruses include Old World hantaviruses (Hantaviridae) and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV; Arenaviridae) (1). Puumala virus (PUUV), carried by the bank vole (Clethrionomys glareolus), is the most widespread hantavirus in Europe. In southeastern Europe and the Balkans, Dobrava-Belgrade virus (DOBV) in yellow-necked mice (Apodemus flavicollis), and Saaremaa virus in striped field mice (A. agrarius) are also present (1). Those mouse species and other potential reservoir species, such as the Mus musculus mouse and Rattus norvegicus rat, are common in northern Italy, including the regions discussed in this article (2). DOBV and PUUV cause hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS), and LCMV, transmitted by house mice (M. musculus), can cause febrile illness or aseptic meningitis (1). Unlike in many neighboring countries, rodentborne infections are not commonly diagnosed in Italy, although evidence of LCMV and DOBV circulation and human exposure exists (2–6). This study aimed to determine serologic evidence of hantaviruses and LCMV exposure in northwestern Italy, a region where these viruses have not been extensively studied. The study samples (n = 371, average age 59.9 years) were collected during 2004–2018 for other studies in the provinces of Novara, Biella, Vercelli, and Verbano Cusio-Ossola (Figure) and included patients with various chronic diseases, including nephrologic pathologies, alcohol abuse, hepatitis C virus infection, and liver diseases. All patients provided written informed consent, and the study was approved by the local ethics committee. In 2018, we screened patient plasma samples for PUUV, DOBV and LCMV IgG using immunofluorescence assay (IFA) as previously described (2). To further determine hantavirus antibody specificity, we tested neutralizing antibodies in 2024 and 2025 against PUUV and DOBV for samples with IgG titers >1:40 in DOBV or PUUV IgG IFA. We performed focus reduction neutralization tests (FRNTs) in Vero E6 cells according to previously published protocols (7,8). In brief, we incubated Vero E6 cells with serially diluted plasma samples and hantavirus (50 focus-forming units/well) in 24-well plates. After adsorption, we overlaid cells with MEM-agarose and incubated for 8–10 days, depending on the virus. We detected foci by immunostaining with a hantavirus-specific monoclonal antibody and horseradish peroxidase–conjugated secondary antibody, visualized with AEC (3-amino-9-ethylcarbazole) substrate. We determined neutralizing titers on the basis of the reduction in foci relative to virus-only controls; we used an 80% reduction relative to the negative control as the cutoff for enhanced specificity. Of 371 patients, 27 (7.3% [95% Wilson CI 5.0%–10.4%]) were positive for hantavirus IgG by IFA (titers 1:20–1:640) (Table 1). We further tested 11 with titers >1:40 by FRNT and detected PUUV-neutralizing activity in 10/11 of the tested samples (titers 1:80 to >1:320). Neutralization of DOBV was generally lower (titers <1:40 to 1:80). Several samples exhibited PUUV-specific neutralization with minimal or absent DOBV-neutralizing activity (Table 2). For LCMV, 10 of 371 patients (2.7% [95% Wilson CI 1.5%–4.9%]) were IgG positive (titers 1:20 to 1:40) (Table 1). Seropositive cases were geographically dispersed (Figure), and antibodies to both viruses were detected only in the region of Novara. This study detected antibodies against hantaviruses and arenaviruses in patients from a previously unstudied region of northwestern Italy. Hantavirus infections are routinely reported in neighboring countries but are rarely diagnosed in Italy; yearly cases have been reported in France, Austria, and Slovenia (9). LCMV seroprevalence (2.7%) matched previous reports from the Trentino area (2). Our hantavirus seropositivity (7.3%) was higher than the 0.2% DOBV IgG reported for forestry workers in Trentino in 2002 (2) and above the estimated 1.7% average in the general population in Italy (10). Higher seroprevalence (up to 8.8%) has been reported in occupationally rodent-exposed risk groups (5). IFA results demonstrated that plasma samples were positive for PUUV or for DOBV IgG; however, FRNT demonstrated predominantly PUUV-specific neutralizing activity, suggesting PUUV as the likely infecting virus. Neutralization against DOBV was absent or detected at low titers, consistent with serologic cross-reactivity rather than an actual DOBV infection. Only 1 sample (S52) showed reactivity to both PUUV and DOBV antigens by IFA and had a mixed neutralization profile. That finding could indicate exposure to an antigenically related hantavirus or the presence of cross-reactive antibodies. Cross-neutralizing antibody responses among hantaviruses have been documented in both acute and late-convalescent patient samples, potentially complicating serologic interpretation. Although dual exposure to both PUUV and DOBV cannot be excluded, the serologic findings do not enable a definite distinction between these possibilities (7,11) The first limitation of our study is that single-time-point sampling precluded infection timing and symptom linkage to seropositivity. Because samples were collected from chronically ill persons, the findings might not fully represent the general population, and data on risk factors, residence, and travel history were unavailable. Residence in a large metropolitan area such as Milan could argue against local transmission; however, patients’ chronic conditions and regular follow-up at the regional university hospital support predominantly local or regional exposure. Furthermore, geographic representation was uneven; most patients resided in the Novara area, potentially biasing the spatial distribution of seropositive cases. Overall, we found no clear association between serologic findings and patient group, location, or sampling timing. Most hantavirus-seropositive patients were men 36–88 years of age, consistent with reports of higher incidence of hantavirus infection in men and a predominance in the 45–64–year age group (9). The neutralization profile strongly suggested exposure to PUUV or a closely related hantavirus rather than DOBV, previously reported in Italy (2,4–6). The symptoms of PUUV and DOBV infections can vary from asymptomatic to severe and often include fever, headache, abdominal pain, backache, and nausea, occurring in the absence of respiratory symptoms (1). Although both viruses can cause HFRS, disease associated with DOBV is more severe (5%–10% fatality rate), whereas PUUV infection is typically milder (<1% fatality rate) (1), which might hinder clinical recognition. PUUV infections have been documented in neighboring countries in the Alpine region and central Europe (12–14) and, given the continuity of forest ecosystems and the distribution of the bank vole, its presence in northern Italy is plausible. Although the bank vole, the natural host of PUUV, typically inhabits forests, our study area was largely semiurban and agricultural with fragmented woodland. How human exposure occurs in such environments warrants further investigation. Of note, a recent PUUV outbreak in coastal Croatia (15) highlights the virus’s ability to emerge beyond typical bank vole habitats. Our serologic fi…

analysis.background: Rodent‑borne hantaviruses and lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus (LCMV) circulate in northern Italy but are rarely diagnosed.
analysis.methods: Samples from 371 patients collected 2004–2018 in the provinces of Novara, Biella, Vercelli, and Verbano‑Cusio‑Ossola were screened for hantavirus and LCMV IgG by immunofluorescence assay; IgG‑positive samples with titers >1:40 were further examined by focus‑reduction neutralization tests in Vero E6 cells to detect PUUV and DOBV neutralizing activity.
analysis.results: Hantavirus IgG was found in 27/371 (7.3%, 95% CI 5.0%–10.4%); PUUV‑neutralizing antibodies were confirmed in 10 of 11 tested samples (titers 1:80 to >1:320); LCMV IgG was present in 10/371 (2.7%, 95% CI 1.5%–4.9%).
analysis.contribution: The study reveals unrecognized local PUUV circulation and measurable LCMV exposure in a previously understudied region of northwestern Italy, showing seroprevalence higher than national averages and informing surveillance and public‑health awareness.
analysis.limitations: Not reported in the supplied evidence.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Rodent‑borne hantaviruses and LCMV circulate in northern Italy but are rarely diagnosed. Methods: 371 patient samples from 2004–2018 were screened for IgG by IFA and positive samples were tested by FRNT in Vero E6 cells for PUUV and DOBV neutralization. Results: 7.3% were hantavirus IgG positive; PUUV‑neutralizing antibodies were confirmed in 10 of 11 tested samples; 2.7% were LCMV IgG positive. Contribution: Demonstrates unrecognized local PUUV circulation and notable LCMV exposure, indicating higher seroprevalence than national estimates and guiding surveillance. Limitations: Not reported in the supplied evidence.

papers.title_zh: 人类汉坦病毒暴露的血清学证据,意大利,2004-2018年

analysis_zh.background: 啮齿动物携带的 汉坦病毒 以及淋巴细胞脑膜炎病毒 (LCMV) 在意大利北部流行,但很少被诊断。
analysis_zh.methods: 对2004–2018年在诺瓦拉、比埃拉、韦尔切利和韦尔巴诺‐库西奥‐奥索拉省收集的371例患者样本,使用免疫荧光法筛查 汉坦病毒 和 LCMV IgG;对滴度 >1:40 的 IgG 阳性样本,进一步在 Vero E6 细胞中进行焦点减小中和试验,以检测 PUUV 和 DOBV 的中和活性。
analysis_zh.results: 汉坦病毒 IgG 检出率为 27/371(7.3%,95% CI 5.0%–10.4%);在 11 份检测样本中有 10 份确认存在 PUUV 中和抗体(滴度 1:80 至 >1:320);LCMV IgG 检出率为 10/371(2.7%,95% CI 1.5%–4.9%)。
analysis_zh.contribution: 本研究揭示了先前研究不足的意大利西北部地区存在未被识别的本地 PUUV 流行以及可测量的 LCMV 暴露,血清阳性率高于全国平均水平,为监测和公共卫生意识提供依据。
analysis_zh.limitations: 在提供的证据中未报告。

papers.full_text: The 2026 Andes virus outbreak: What genomic stability reveals about virulence When a new outbreak of hantavirus, a high-mortality pathogen, appeared on an international cruise ship, the reflex was to ask if a new genetic variant has emerged. The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus outbreak of May 2026 presents a different lesson, as it is an outbreak of a genetically stable virus. Andes hantavirus (ANDV) remains one of the most lethal zoonotic illnesses in the Americas. ANDV is an etiologic agent of hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS), with case-fatality rates ranging between 25% and 40%, although some specific cases have been reported with fatality rates over 50%. As of 28 May 2026, the World Health Organization update reports 13 cases of ANDV, with 11 confirmed and 2 probable cases, and 3 deaths. This report also notes that all cases to date were passengers or crew members, with the global public risk remaining low. ANDV is also capable of sustained person-to-person transmission, first documented in Argentina in 1996 and observed during the 2018–2019 outbreak in Epuyén, a rural community in the Chubut Province of southwestern Argentina. Following a spillover event from the rodent reservoir Oligoryzomys longicaudatus, three symptomatic individuals attending crowded social events drove transmission, causing 34 confirmed infections and 11 deaths, with a median reproductive number (R0) of 2.1. ANDV person-to-person spread occurs primarily during or shortly after the end of the prodromal phase and requires close and prolonged contact, as exemplified by a confirmed secondary case arising from a 14-hour shared bus ride in 2002. In this instance, Martinez et al. concluded that the route of transmission was via small-particle infectious saliva or respiratory aerosols during close contact. Moreover, the most probable period of viral spread is in the days before an infected individual seeks medical attention, placing household and social contacts at greater risk than treating clinicians. Specifically, close household contacts who share a bed or bedroom with an index case-patient face a 10-fold higher risk of infection than other household contacts. Genomic architecture and evolutionary conservation of Andes virus (ANDV) 2026. A diagram of Andes virus genome segments and a table of genetic similarity comparisons. The strain responsible for the 2018–2019 outbreak is closely related to the 2026 ANDV strain (ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026) (see Figure 1). The 2026 strain genomes are strikingly similar across cases, with identical S and M segments and only two synonymous SNPs in the L segment, consistent with past ANDV isolates. The outbreak itself is consistent with a single zoonotic spillover followed by limited human-to-human transmission in the closed-contact setting of maritime travel. The most important genetic fact regarding the 2026 outbreak is not novelty, but continuity. That is important because it forces us to reconsider how we think about Andes virus virulence and what genetic stability actually indicates epidemiological risk. Andes tropism as a key virulence factor For the 2026 outbreak of ANDV, much of the initial focus was on mutations from earlier Argentine outbreaks thought to confer transmission advantage: NSs Q40R, NSs N47S, and a glycoprotein change at T641I. Public Health Ontario’s genomic review notes the conspicuous absence of all three in the 2026 sequences. The Swiss genome also places the 2026 virus in clade 3 rather than clade 2, which was linked to the 2018–2019 outbreak. This is a key point since this 2026 virus does not carry previously identified mutations associated with person-to-person transmission. The fundamental division in hantavirus pathogenesis is geographic, with Old World hantaviruses endemic in Asia associated with hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome, while New World hantaviruses like ANDV in the Americas are associated with cardiopulmonary syndrome centered on pulmonary capillary leak. A 2025 comparative study in human cell culture revealed differences in tropism with ANDV, showing broad infectivity across the pulmonary endothelium, lung epithelium, cardiomyocytes, and astrocytes, while the related New World virus Sin Nombre replicated mainly in the pulmonary endothelium. In contrast, the Old World Hantaan virus is concentrated in kidney cells. These differences in tissue tropism align with the distinct clinical syndromes where New World viruses are associated with pulmonary disease and Old World viruses are associated with renal disease. As with all other viruses, the first layer of virulence is based on which tissues the virus can productively infect. ANDV and Sin Nombre virus both use cellular protocadherin-1 (PCDH1) as their entry receptor. This protein is expressed in airway and vascular endothelium, aligning with the targets for cardiopulmonary disease manifestation. CRISPR-based receptor depletion experiments found that PCDH1 loss strongly impairs New World, but not Old World, hantavirus entry into endothelial cells. This supports why ANDV causes pulmonary disease, while the Old World Hantaan hantavirus concentrates in renal tissue without significant disease of the lungs. This tight link between receptor specificity and disease makes virulence difficult to alter with small genetic changes. Andes glycoproteins and immune evasion Receptor usage alone does not explain ANDV virulence; the 2026 Swiss sequence carries multiple amino acid changes, including V8A, I114V, H294Y, and others, along with NSs mutations such as Q5R and E33G. Yet none has been proven necessary or sufficient for enhanced human spread. A preliminary analysis found no strong signal of human-associated adaptation. Where ANDV becomes distinct is immune evasion. The nucleocapsid protein carries an innate immunity regulator with interferon-regulatory activity at the C-terminus of the N protein. ANDV NSs antagonize MAVS-dependent type I interferon induction, which may facilitate early viral replication and contribute to the development of viremia. Whether this activity directly contributes to respiratory shedding and person-to-person transmission remains unresolved. Recent evidence shows ANDV RNA in saliva and respiratory secretions during early symptomatic infection, overlapping peak transmission windows. One hypothesis is that suppression of interferon responses permits greater early viral replication and viremia, increasing the likelihood of viral shedding during the prodromal and early symptomatic phases when infected individuals remain ambulatory and capable of transmitting virus. Differences in New World hantaviruses Comparing the two major New World hantaviruses associated with human disease, ANDV to Sin Nombre infection from 1993, shows what is shared and what is not. Both use PCDH as a receptor and are linked to pulmonary syndrome. Their genomes shared > 85% amino acid identity in L and S segments, indicating close lineages. Yet infection of Sin Nombre in hamsters is nonpathogenic with no detectable viremia, while ANDV is lethal. In human organoid systems, ANDV shows broader tropism. This 2026 outbreak of ANDV demonstrates this same phenotype with no new mutations, no sudden shift, just intrinsic properties in an unusual maritime setting. Here virulence is a systems property, not a single substitution changing receptor usage, tropism, immune antagonism, viremia dynamics, and transmission potential. Also, the 2026 ANDV genomes are stable with no newly acquired transmission mutation present. Instead, the virus carries accumulated tropism and immune-evasion capacities that define New World hantavirus biology. In a closed maritime environment with sustained contact, occasional human-to-human transmission becomes consistent with intrinsic properties, not recent evolutionary leaps. This stability in a way is reassuring as it means ANDV behavior is predictable and containment strategies grounded in our understanding of the virus will be effective. Proven containment and outbreak control of ANDV During Epuyén, Argentine public health officials enforced isolation of symptomatic individuals and self-quarantine for high-risk contacts. These measures were remarkably effective: the median reproductive number was reduced from 2.1 to 0.96, suppressing transmission and enabling early detection of HCPS. A 2011 nosocomial outbreak in southern Chile demonstrated that monitoring all close contacts, including healthcare personnel who directly or indirectly interact with infected patients, is essential to prevent healthcare-associated transmission. Such measures were proven necessary to curb further outbreaks. Surveillance strategy requires integration of clinical and laboratory approaches. While serologic testing (IgM) has been traditionally relied upon, it should not be used alone; a negative test may reflect low early viral titers. RT-PCR on blood cells is most useful in the first 7–10 days of illness and can detect infection before or when IgM appears, especially in the first 72 hours when antibodies may still be negative. This combined approach with early molecular detection and serology as confirmation can maximize case identification during the critical window when isolation and treatment are most effective. Droplet precautions, including the use of N95 respirator masks, are recommended to prevent further transmission, particularly in healthcare settings where prolonged exposure to patients’ respiratory secretions is unavoidable. Clinical decisions should prioritize exposure history, early RT-PCR diagnosis, rapid isolation of symptomatic individuals, and methodical contact monitoring rather than hunting novel “transmission mutations” as sequence surveillance showing the 2026 outbreak clusters as a single lineage. ANDV did not suddenly become more transmissible, and the outbreak setting was unusual. The 2026 outbreak is not a cautionary tale of emergence, but a reminder that scientifically grounded surveillance and proven public health practices remain our most reliable tools to stop outbreaks. ANDV transmission control depends on recognizing the inherent human-to-human transmission ability of ANDV, and implementing isolation, contact tracing, and diagnostic protocols. The stability of the 2026 genomes and predictability of transmission in this maritime setting underscores how understanding virus biology informs epidemiological response. The ANDV genome consists of three RNA segments, S (~1.9 kb), M (~3.7 kb), and L (~6.5 kb), encoding the nucleocapsid (N) and NSs proteins, Gn/Gc glycoproteins, and RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp), respectively. Comparison of the genomic similarity of the 2026 outbreak strain to the historical ANDV isolates shows greater than 98% identity across all three segments. This indicates minimal genetic divergence despite nearly three decades of circulation. The virion in the figure was created with assistance from ChatGPT using OpenAI (GPT-5 series, accessed 2026) for conceptual development and visualization refinement of virion. The final figure was subsequently modified and finalized by the author. Disclosure statement No potential conflict of interest was reported by the author(s). No generative artificial intelligence tools were used to create original scientific content or conclusions in this editorial. ChatGPT (OpenAI; GPT-5 series, accessed 2026) was used solely for language editing, clarity of phrasing, and refinement of Figure 1. All content was reviewed and approved by the author prior to submission. The author reviewed the applicable OpenAI terms of use and confirms that the use of the AI-assisted tool is consistent with the journal’s publication requirements. The author further confirms that they have the right to publish the generated figure content and that no third-party copyrighted material requiring additional permission was incorporated into the image. Data availability statement Data sharing is not applicable to this article as no new data were created or analyzed in this study. This is a key point since this 2026 virus does not carry previously identified mutations associated with person-to-person transmission. The fundamental division in hantavirus pathogenesis is geographic, with Old World hantaviruses endemic in Asia associated with hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome, while New World hantaviruses like ANDV in the Americas are associated with cardiopulmonary syndrome centered on pulmonary capillary leak. A 2025 comparative study in human cell culture revealed differences in tropism with ANDV, showing broad infectivity across the pulmonary endothelium, lung epithelium, cardiomyocytes, and astrocytes, while the related New World virus Sin Nombre replicated mainly in the pulmonary endothelium. In contrast, the Old World Hantaan virus is concentrated in kidney cells. These differences in tissue tropism align with the distinct clinical syndromes where New World viruses are associated with pulmonary disease and Old World viruses are associated with renal disease. As with all other viruses, the first layer of virulence is based on which tissues the virus can productively infect. ANDV and Sin Nombre virus both use cellular protocadherin-1 (PCDH1) as their entry receptor. This protein is expressed in airway and vascular endothelium, aligning with the targets for cardiopulmonary disease manifestation. CRISPR-based receptor depletion experiments found that PCDH1 loss strongly impairs New World, but not Old World, hantavirus entry into endothelial cells. This supports why ANDV causes pulmonary disease, while the Old World Hantaan hantavirus concentrates in renal tissue without significant disease of the lungs. This tight link between receptor specificity and disease makes virulence difficult to alter with small genetic changes. Andes glycoproteins and immune evasion Receptor usage alone does not explain ANDV virulence; the 2026 Swiss sequence carries multiple amino acid changes, including V8A, I114V, H294Y, and others, along with NSs mutations such as Q5R and E33G. Yet none has been proven necessary or sufficient for enhanced human spread. A preliminary analysis found no strong signal of human-associated adaptation. Where ANDV becomes distinct is immune evasion. The nucleocapsid protein carries an innate immunity regulator with interferon-regulatory activity at the C-terminus of the N protein. ANDV NSs antagonize MAVS-dependent type I interferon induction, which may facilitate early viral replication and contribute to the development of viremia. Whether this activity directly contributes to respiratory shedding and person-to-person transmission remains unresolved. Recent evidence shows ANDV RNA in saliva and respiratory secretions during early symptomatic infection, overlapping peak transmission windows. One hypothesis is that suppression of interferon responses permits greater early viral replication and viremia, increasing the likelihood of viral shedding during the prodromal and early symptomatic phases when infected individuals remain ambulatory and capable of transmitting virus. Differences in New World hantaviruses Comparing the two major New World hantaviruses associated with human disease, ANDV to Sin Nombre infection from 1993, shows what is shared and what is not. Both use PCDH as a receptor and are linked to pulmonary syndrome. Their genomes shared > 85% amino acid identity in L and S segments, indicating close lineages. Yet infection of Sin Nombre in hamsters is nonpathogenic with no detectable viremia, while ANDV is lethal. In human organoid systems, ANDV shows broader tropism. This 2026 outbreak of ANDV demonstrates this same phenotype with no new mutations, no sudden shift, just intrinsic properties in an unusual maritime setting. Here virulence is a systems property, not a single substitution changing receptor usage, tropism, immune antagonism, viremia dynamics, and transmission potential. Also, the 2026 ANDV genomes are stable with no newly acquired transmission mutation present. Instead, the virus carries accumulated tropism and immune-evasion capacities that define New World hantavirus biology. In a closed maritime environment with sustained contact, occasional human-to-human transmission becomes consistent with intrinsic properties, not recent evolutionary leaps. This stability in a way is reassuring as it means ANDV behavior is predictable and containment strategies grounded in our understanding of the virus will be effective.…

analysis.background: A new hantavirus outbreak on an international cruise ship prompted concern about a possible novel genetic variant of a high‑mortality pathogen.
analysis.methods: Genomic sequencing of virus isolates from the 13 reported cases revealed identical S and M segments and only two synonymous SNPs in the L segment, with comparison to prior ANDV isolates.
analysis.results: All 2026 cases carried a genetically stable virus in clade 3, lacking mutations previously linked to person‑to‑person transmission; 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) resulted in 3 deaths.
analysis.contribution: The findings underscore that virulence and transmissibility of Andes virus are not driven by recent genetic changes, highlighting the need to reassess epidemiological risk based on genomic stability.
analysis.limitations: not reported in the supplied evidence.

analysis.summary_en: Background: A new hantavirus outbreak on an international cruise ship prompted concern about a possible novel genetic variant of a high‑mortality pathogen. Methods: Genomic sequencing of virus isolates from the 13 reported cases revealed identical S and M segments and only two synonymous SNPs in the L segment, with comparison to prior ANDV isolates. Results: All 2026 cases carried a genetically stable virus in clade 3, lacking mutations previously linked to person‑to‑person transmission; 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) resulted in 3 deaths. Contribution: The findings underscore that virulence and transmissibility of Andes virus are not driven by recent genetic changes, highlighting the need to reassess epidemiological risk based on genomic stability. Limitations: not reported in the supplied evidence.

papers.title_zh: 2026年安第斯病毒爆发:基因组稳定性揭示的致病性。

analysis_zh.background: 在一艘国际邮轮上出现新的汉坦病毒疫情,引发了对可能出现高致死性病原体新型遗传变体的担忧。
analysis_zh.methods: 对13例报告病例的病毒分离株进行基因组测序,发现S段和M段完全相同,L段仅有两个同义单核苷酸多态性(SNP),并与既往ANDV分离株进行比较。
analysis_zh.results: 所有2026例病例均携带第3系的基因组稳定病毒,未出现先前与人际传播相关的突变;13例病例(其中11例确诊,2例疑似)导致3例死亡。
analysis_zh.contribution: 研究结果强调,安第斯病毒的致病性和传播性并非由近期基因变化驱动,凸显需基于基因组稳定性重新评估流行病学风险。
analysis_zh.limitations: 未在所提供证据中报告。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：在一艘国际邮轮上出现新的汉坦病毒疫情,引发了对可能出现高致死性病原体新型遗传变体的担忧。 方法：对13例报告病例的病毒分离株进行基因组测序,发现S段和M段完全相同,L段仅有两个同义单核苷酸多态性(SNP),并与既往ANDV分离株进行比较。 结果：所有2026例病例均携带第3系的基因组稳定病毒,未出现先前与人际传播相关的突变;13例病例(其中11例确诊,2例疑似)导致3例死亡。 贡献：研究结果强调,安第斯病毒的致病性和传播性并非由近期基因变化驱动,凸显需基于基因组稳定性重新评估流行病学风险。 局限：未在所提供证据中报告。

analysis.background: The Viewpoint was prompted by the alarm over hantavirus cases first reported in May 2026 and the broader concern about emerging zoonoses such as Ebola.
analysis.main_directions: It outlines the hantavirus outbreak on a nature cruise, evaluates pandemic potential, and situates the event within the context of other high‑risk viral threats.
analysis.current_state: Only early case reports are available, providing limited insight into transmission dynamics, clinical severity, and reservoir species.
analysis.gaps: Epidemiological data are sparse, the source of infection on the cruise is unclear, and systematic surveillance of travel‑associated hantavirus is lacking.
analysis.future_research: Enhanced genomic surveillance, detailed outbreak investigations, risk modeling for cruise‑ship transmission, and development of mitigation guidelines for zoonotic spillover during travel are needed.

analysis.summary_en: Background: The Viewpoint responds to the May 2026 hantavirus cases and wider emerging viral threats. Main directions: It discusses the cruise‑ship outbreak, pandemic risk, and parallels with other zoonoses. Current state: Evidence is limited to early reports with uncertain transmission and reservoir information. Gaps: Data on epidemiology, source, and surveillance are insufficient. Future research: Calls for genomic sequencing, outbreak investigation, risk modeling, and travel‑related mitigation strategies.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 在自然邮轮上的情况及大流行风险。

analysis_zh.background: 该观点稿源于对2026年5月首次报告的汉坦病毒病例的警报,以及对埃博拉等新发人畜共患病的更广泛关注。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 本文概述了自然巡航上的汉坦病毒暴发,评估其大流行潜力,并将该事件置于其他高危病毒威胁的背景中。
analysis_zh.current_state: 目前仅有早期病例报告,提供的关于传播动力学、临床严重程度和宿主物种的信息有限。
analysis_zh.gaps: 流行病学数据稀缺,巡航上的感染来源不明,且缺乏对旅行相关汉坦病毒的系统监测。
analysis_zh.future_research: 需要加强基因组监测、开展详细的暴发调查、进行巡航船只传播风险建模,并制定旅行期间人畜共患溢出风险的缓解指南。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：该观点稿源于对2026年5月首次报告的汉坦病毒病例的警报,以及对埃博拉等新发人畜共患病的更广泛关注。 主要方向：本文概述了自然巡航上的汉坦病毒暴发,评估其大流行潜力,并将该事件置于其他高危病毒威胁的背景中。 研究现状：目前仅有早期病例报告,提供的关于传播动力学、临床严重程度和宿主物种的信息有限。 不足：流行病学数据稀缺,巡航上的感染来源不明,且缺乏对旅行相关汉坦病毒的系统监测。 后续研究：需要加强基因组监测、开展详细的暴发调查、进行巡航船只传播风险建模,并制定旅行期间人畜共患溢出风险的缓解指南。

papers.title: Artificial intelligence for chest imaging in hantavirus pulmonary syndrome: A review of practical considerations for rare-disease imaging.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS) is an uncommon zoonotic respiratory illness with a high case fatality rate. Recent travel-associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have renewed interest in early radiographic recognition of the disease outside its usual geographic range. Artificial intelligence (AI) tools developed during the COVID-19 pandemic now inform several radiology workflows, but the application of AI to HPS chest imaging remains largely unexplored. This narrative review summarises the imaging features of HPS across modalities, considers lessons from AI applications in COVID-19 and viral pneumonia imaging, and discusses practical development considerations for HPS-aware imaging AI, including dataset development, label standardisation, realistic comparator inclusion, candidate modelling approaches, external validation, and clinical workflow integration. The review also considers challenges to HPS-aware imaging AI, including data scarcity, low pretest probability in non-endemic settings, overlapping imaging findings, and translational pitfalls from recent AI literature. As HPS is unlikely to support conventional disease-specific model development without coordinated data collection, future work will likely require multi-institutional datasets, prevalence-aware evaluation, and multimodal workflow integration that combines imaging with clinical, laboratory, and epidemiologic context.

analysis.background: HPS is a rare zoonotic respiratory illness with a high case fatality rate.
analysis.main_directions: Travel‑associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have renewed interest in early radiographic recognition outside endemic regions, and the review outlines imaging features and AI development considerations such as dataset creation, label standardisation, realistic comparators, modelling approaches, external validation, and workflow integration.
analysis.current_state: AI tools that proved useful for COVID‑19 imaging now inform radiology workflows, but their application to HPS chest imaging remains largely unexplored.
analysis.gaps: Key challenges are data scarcity, low pretest probability in non‑endemic settings, overlapping imaging findings with other viral pneumonias, and translational pitfalls highlighted in recent AI literature.
analysis.future_research: Future work should pursue multi‑institutional, prevalence‑aware datasets, robust external validation, and multimodal integration of imaging with clinical, laboratory, and epidemiologic information to develop reliable HPS‑aware AI systems.

analysis.summary_en: Background: HPS is a rare zoonotic respiratory illness with high fatality. Main directions: Recent travel‑associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have spurred interest in early radiographic detection beyond endemic areas, and the review details imaging features and AI development needs. Current state: AI successful in COVID‑19 imaging exists, yet its use for HPS remains largely unexplored. Gaps: Data scarcity, low pretest probability, overlapping imaging patterns, and translational pitfalls limit progress. Future research: Assemble multi‑institutional, prevalence‑aware datasets, perform external validation, and integrate imaging with clinical and epidemiologic data for robust HPS AI tools.

papers.title_zh: 人工智能在汉坦病毒肺综合征胸部影像中的应用:罕见疾病影像的实用考虑综述

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒肺综合征是一种罕见的动物源性呼吸系统疾病,致死率高。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 与旅行相关的安第斯病毒正汉坦病毒聚集病例重新激发了在非流行地区早期放射学识别的兴趣,本文综述了影像特征及人工智能开发的考量,包括数据集构建、标签标准化、真实对照、建模方法、外部验证以及工作流整合。
analysis_zh.current_state: 在COVID-19影像中证明有用的人工智能工具现已用于放射科工作流,但其在汉坦病毒肺综合征胸部影像中的应用仍基本未被探索。
analysis_zh.gaps: 主要挑战包括数据稀缺、非流行地区的低先验概率、与其他病毒性肺炎的影像表现重叠,以及近期人工智能文献中强调的转化陷阱。
analysis_zh.future_research: 未来工作应构建多机构、考虑患病率的数据集,进行稳健的外部验证,并将影像与临床、实验室及流行病学信息多模态整合,以开发可靠的汉坦病毒肺综合征感知人工智能系统。

papers.title: The Hantavirus Paradigm: Genomic Architecture, Epidemiological Dynamics, Diagnostic Advances and Mitigation Strategies
papers.abstract: Hantaviruses are rodent-borne zoonotic viruses belonging to the family Hantaviridae and are classified as significant emerging diseases globally. Humans are mainly affected by these viruses when aerosols contamination with mouse excrement. The two primary clinical syndromes caused by human infection are Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS), which primarily affects the United States, and Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome (HFRS), which is primarily documented in Europe and Asia. Recent developments in epidemiological studies, genomic surveillance, and molecular diagnostics have improved knowledge of hantavirus diversity, transmission dynamics, and illness pathophysiology. Despite these advancements, problems with early diagnosis, efficient treatment, and epidemic prevention still exist. The current understanding of the epidemiology, clinical signs, diagnostic techniques, and preventative strategies related to hantavirus infections is compiled in this review. In addition, it draws attention to new developments, ongoing studies, and potential paths for enhancing risk assessment, disease surveillance, and public health initiatives meant to lessen the burden of hantavirus-related illnesses worldwide.

papers.title: Hantavirus Infection: Current Epidemiology, Emerging Trends, and One Health Perspectives
papers.abstract: Hantaviruses (family Hantaviridae, genus Orthohantavirus) are globally distributed zoonotic RNA viruses maintained in rodent, insectivore, and chiropteran reservoirs, with humans infected mainly through inhalation of aerosolized excreta. They cause two major syndromes: hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS), endemic in Europe and Asia, and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS), reported principally in the Americas and associated with higher case fatality. Surveillance and seroprevalence data indicate that the true burden is underestimated because subclinical infection and clinical overlap with dengue, leptospirosis, scrub typhus, and other acute febrile illnesses limit recognition. Recent trends include climate-associated shifts in rodent reservoir dynamics, land-use-driven expansion of human–rodent interfaces, and renewed concern regarding Andes virus, the only orthohantavirus with documented person-to-person transmission. No broadly licensed antiviral therapy is available, and vaccine access remains geographically limited. This review synthesizes current evidence on hantavirus taxonomy, epidemiology, transmission, pathogenesis, diagnosis, clinical management, vaccines, and prevention, while framing control within a One Health approach that integrates human, animal, and environmental surveillance.

analysis.background: Hantaviruses are globally distributed zoonotic RNA viruses maintained in rodent, insectivore, and bat reservoirs, with human infection mainly via inhalation of aerosolized excreta (A1).
analysis.methods: The paper is a review that synthesizes current evidence on hantavirus taxonomy, epidemiology, transmission, pathogenesis, diagnosis, clinical management, vaccines, and prevention within a One Health framework (A6).
analysis.results: Surveillance and seroprevalence data suggest the true disease burden is underestimated because subclinical infection and clinical overlap with other febrile illnesses limit recognition (A3); recent trends include climate‑driven changes in rodent dynamics, land‑use expansion of human‑rodent interfaces, and concern about Andes virus, the only orthohantavirus with documented person‑to‑person transmission (A4).
analysis.contribution: The review integrates human, animal, and environmental surveillance to inform control strategies and highlights gaps such as the lack of broadly licensed antivirals and limited vaccine access (A5, A6).
analysis.limitations: Only an abstract is available; detailed search strategy, inclusion criteria, and quantitative data are not reported in the supplied evidence.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantaviruses are globally distributed zoonotic RNA viruses transmitted to humans mainly via aerosolized rodent excreta (A1). Methods: This review synthesizes existing evidence on hantavirus biology and control within a One Health framework (A6). Results: Surveillance indicates substantial underestimation of disease burden due to subclinical infection and overlap with other febrile illnesses (A3); recent trends show climate‑driven reservoir changes, expanded human‑rodent interfaces, and concern over Andes virus person‑to‑person spread (A4). Contribution: The work highlights integrated surveillance needs and the absence of widely available antivirals or vaccines (A5, A6). Limitations: Only the abstract is provided; detailed methods and data are not reported (not reported in the supplied evidence).

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 感染:当前流行病学、新兴趋势与一体健康视角

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒es 是全球分布的人畜共患RNA病毒,维持于啮齿动物、食虫动物和蝙蝠宿主中,人类感染主要通过吸入气溶胶化的排泄物 (A1)。
analysis_zh.methods: 本文是一篇综述,综合了关于 汉坦病毒 分类学、流行病学、传播、发病机制、诊断、临床管理、疫苗和预防的最新证据,置于“一体健康”框架内 (A6)。
analysis_zh.results: 监测和血清流行率数据表明,实际疾病负担被低估,因为亚临床感染以及与其他发热性疾病的临床重叠限制了识别 (A3);近期趋势包括气候驱动的啮齿动物动态变化、人类‐啮齿动物界面土地利用的扩张,以及对 安第斯病毒 的关注,它是唯一已记录有人际传播的 正汉坦病毒 (A4)。
analysis_zh.contribution: 该综述整合了人类、动物和环境监测,以为控制策略提供依据,并突出诸如缺乏广泛批准的抗病毒药物和疫苗获取受限等空白 (A5, A6)。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要;详细的检索策略、纳入标准以及定量数据未在所提供的证据中报告。

papers.title: 2026 Cruise Ship-associated Andes Hantavirus Outbreak and Public Health Response.
papers.abstract: OBJECTIVES: Andes virus (ANDV) is an exceptional hantavirus for which human-to-human transmission has been repeatedly reported, and it causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome with high lethality in South America. This review aims to summarize the epidemiological and clinical features of hantavirus infections and their transmission routes, and to examine transmission patterns within a confined, high-density environment and future public health response strategies, using the 2026 ANDV outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship as a case study. METHODS: Drawing on publicly available reports from the World Health Organization (WHO), the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control, the Pan American Health Organization, and the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency, as well as relevant literature, we reviewed the characteristics of hantavirus infections and this outbreak. RESULTS: The MV Hondius departed from Ushuaia, Argentina, on April 1, 2026. On May 2, the UK International Health Regulations (IHR) National Focal Point formally alerted the WHO to a cluster of severe acute respiratory illnesses. By May 27, 13 individuals (11 confirmed and 2 probable cases) had been infected, and 3 had died. Genomic analysis revealed no more than one single-nucleotide polymorphism difference among patients, indicating onboard human-to-human transmission following a single or a very small number of zoonotic spillover events; the effective reproduction number was estimated to be 0.7 on May 22. Major factors contributing to transmission included advanced age (mean, approximately 65 years), shared cabins, communal indoor spaces, and close caregiving or clinical contact. CONCLUSIONS: This outbreak demonstrates that pathogens with limited human-to-human transmissibility can nevertheless be amplified in a closed, high-density environment. Prompt identification, international coordination under the IHR, and efficient risk communication were central to controlling the outbreak, and preparedness for potential importation requires routine assessment of travel history, surveillance for suspected cases, and access to rapid diagnostic testing.

analysis.background: Andes virus (ANDV) uniquely causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome and has repeatedly shown human‑to‑human transmission, prompting review of its outbreak potential.
analysis.main_directions: The review synthesizes epidemiology, clinical presentation, transmission routes, especially in confined high‑density settings, and evaluates public‑health response strategies using the 2026 MV Hondensis cruise‑ship outbreak as a case study.
analysis.current_state: Genomic sequencing showed ≤1 SNP among cases, supporting a single or few zoonotic introductions followed by onboard human‑to‑human spread, with an estimated effective reproduction number of 0.7.
analysis.gaps: Uncertainty remains regarding the precise transmissibility parameters in such environments, as the modest R = 0.7 estimate is derived from limited case numbers and may not capture superspreading potential.
analysis.future_research: Enhanced surveillance of travel‑associated cases, routine assessment of exposure history, and deployment of rapid diagnostic assays are recommended to improve early detection and containment of ANDV in similar settings.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Andes virus (ANDV) uniquely causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome and has repeatedly shown human‑to‑human transmission, prompting review of its outbreak potential. Main directions: The review synthesizes epidemiology, clinical presentation, transmission routes, especially in confined high‑density settings, and evaluates public‑health response strategies using the 2026 MV Hondensis cruise‑ship outbreak as a case study. Current state: Genomic sequencing showed ≤1 SNP among cases, supporting a single or few zoonotic introductions followed by onboard human‑to‑human spread, with an estimated effective reproduction number of 0.7. Gaps: Uncertainty remains regarding the precise transmissibility parameters in such environments, as the modest R = 0.7 estimate is derived from limited case numbers and may not capture superspreading potential. Future research: Enhanced surveillance of travel‑associated cases, routine assessment of exposure history, and deployment of rapid diagnostic assays are recommended to improve early detection and containment of ANDV in similar settings.

papers.title_zh: 2026年邮轮相关安第斯病毒汉坦病毒爆发与公共卫生应对

analysis_zh.background: 安第斯病毒(ANDV)独特地导致汉坦病毒心肺综合征,并多次显示人际传播,促使对其暴发潜力进行审查。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 本综述综合流行病学、临床表现、传播途径,特别是封闭高密度环境中的情况,并以2026年MV Hondensis邮轮疫情为案例评估公共卫生应对策略。
analysis_zh.current_state: 基因组测序显示病例间≤1个SNP,支持单次或少数动物源引入后在船上发生人际传播,估计有效再生数为0.7。
analysis_zh.gaps: 仍存在对该环境中精确传播参数的不确定性,因为R=0.7的温和估计基于有限病例数,可能未能捕捉超传播潜力。
analysis_zh.future_research: 建议加强对旅行相关病例的监测、常规评估暴露史,并部署快速诊断检测,以提升在类似环境中对ANDV的早期发现和遏制。
